content:
Case 2:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 24
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: New skin changes on the back and extremities
- Vital signs: BP 120/75 mmHg, HR 116/min, T° 36.8 °C, respiratory rate 16/min.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with new symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine blood panels, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging as first-line tests. Invasive tissue sampling and specialized analyses usually require additional coordination and are not available in this round.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
On June 4, 2025, the patient presented with new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities. She reported intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness. Otherwise, she was in good general condition, afebrile, and showed no signs of skin or mucosal GvHD. A marked psychological stress reaction was noted.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IE 1×/week, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, ruxolitinib 10 mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg orally once daily, three times per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), steroid cream p.r.n.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "skin examination"}}
'history_physical' already provided.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc:
CBC: Hb 14.2 g/dL, WBC 6.8 ×10⁹/L, Plt 240 ×10⁹/L; MCV 89 fL, MCH 30 pg; differential: neutrophils 58%, lymphocytes 32%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "comprehensive metabolic panel"}}

Chemistry:
Chemistry: Na 140 mmol/L, K 4.2 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, BUN 14 mg/dL, AST 22 U/L, ALT 24 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, LDH 180 U/L, uric acid 5.0 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 2.35 mmol/L, NT-proBNP 1250 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.024 ng/mL.

response.request: detailed skin examination findings (morphology, distribution, color, size, any scaling or blistering)

content: 'history_physical' already provided.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "past medical history"}}

Demographics:
Age: 24
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 0
Comorbidities: Psychiatric disturbance, history of alloSCT
Social history: Lives with her partner and one 2-year-old child. Not currently employed; she had begun law studies but paused due to illness.
Height: 164 cm; Bodyweight: 62 kg.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "viral PCR panel for CMV, EBV, HHV-6"}}

'infectious_workup' available in round 2.
The case includes an infectious workup item (viral PCRs, cultures, inflammatory markers), but it is not available in this round. Consider requesting currently available items such as CBC, chemistry, or imaging in this round instead.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "echocardiogram"}}

Echocardiography:
Echocardiogram: Showed tissue thickening in the pericardium and septal hypertrophy, raising suspicion of an unclear mass lesion.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast"}}

'CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast' not available. Request a specific test or report.
This case does not include a CT chest/abdomen/pelvis report. For abdominal imaging you can request 'ultrasound_abdomen' (available now); FDG-PET/CT is also in the case but becomes available in a later round.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "peripheral blood smear review"}}

'peripheral blood smear review' not available. Try 'cbc' instead.
There is no separate “peripheral smear” item listed, but a peripheral smear review is most closely covered by the CBC (includes differential and smear review). You can request the CBC/differential to obtain this information.

response.request: bone marrow biopsy with morphology, flow cytometry, and cytogenetics

content:
Bone Marrow:
June 5, 2025: Morphology: Normocellular bone marrow with age-appropriate cellularity. Trilineage hematopoiesis present with some dysmorphic maturation in erythroid, myeloid, and megakaryocytic lineages, consistent with reactive changes after alloSCT. No excess blasts or abnormal infiltrates were observed.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "FDG-PET/CT scan results"}}

Pet Ct:
FDG-PET/CT (2025-06-07): Revealed multiple FDG-avid cutaneous lesions (SUVmax 6.2) and intra-abdominal lesions adjacent to the left ureter (SUVmax 8.1), accompanied by several mildly FDG-avid mediastinal lymph nodes (SUVmax 4.5).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "skin biopsy pathology report"}}

Skin Biopsy:
Skin biopsy (2025-06-07): The dermis and subcutis are diffusely infiltrated by atypical hematopoietic cells in a perivascular and interstitial distribution. The cells show irregular nuclear contours, vesicular chromatin, and occasional nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent. Immunohistochemistry: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-. Molecular pathology: NPM1 mutation present in cutaneous infiltration.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia (myeloid sarcoma) after allogeneic stem cell transplant", "differentials": ["Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD)", "Metastatic carcinoma", "Viral infection with cutaneous and cardiac involvement", "Drug reaction (e.g., ruxolitinib-induced rash and pericarditis)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Brain MRI with contrast to evaluate for CNS involvement (headaches)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Cardiac MRI with contrast to characterize pericardial/septal mass", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Induction chemotherapy with FLAG-IDA (fludarabine, cytarabine, G-CSF, idarubicin) for relapsed AML", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) consultation and preparation after chemotherapy response", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Intrathecal chemotherapy prophylaxis (methotrexate/cytarabine/hydrocortisone) to prevent CNS relapse", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Supportive care with infection prophylaxis, G-CSF, transfusion support, and cardiac monitoring", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Consider localized radiotherapy to bulky or symptomatic extramedullary lesions (skin, abdominal mass)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Extramedullary relapse of NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia (myeloid sarcoma) after allogeneic stem cell transplant
differentials: Viral infection with cutaneous and cardiac involvement
Drug reaction (e.g., ruxolitinib-induced rash and pericarditis)

treatment_plan.action: Brain MRI with contrast to evaluate for CNS involvement (headaches)
Cardiac MRI with contrast to characterize pericardial/septal mass
Induction chemotherapy with FLAG-IDA (fludarabine, cytarabine, G-CSF, idarubicin) for relapsed AML
Donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) consultation and preparation after chemotherapy response
Intrathecal chemotherapy prophylaxis (methotrexate/cytarabine/hydrocortisone) to prevent CNS relapse
Supportive care with infection prophylaxis, G-CSF, transfusion support, and cardiac monitoring
Consider localized radiotherapy to bulky or symptomatic extramedullary lesions (skin, abdominal mass)

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Prior transplant details including date, donor, conditioning regimen, GVHD history, and indication for ruxolitinib"}}

response.request: Prior transplant details including date, donor, conditioning regimen, GVHD history, and indication for ruxolitinib

content:
Treatment History:
Diagnosis: 2024-02-21, AML with NPM1 mutation (WHO 2022). ELN 2022 risk: favorable.
Initial immunophenotype (February 21, 2024): CD34^low^ blasts expressing CD33, CD14, CD64, CD117, MPO, and CD13, negative for HLA-DR, CD7, CD56, and TdT.
Induction (Cycle 1): “7+3” (cytarabine 200 mg/m² for 7 days (2,352 mg total) + daunorubicin 60 mg/m² for 3 days (303 mg total)) plus gemtuzumab ozogamicin 5 mg on days 1, 4, and 7 (15 mg total). Complications: Neutropenic fever and pneumonia, otherwise well tolerated.
Consolidation (March–July 2024): Four cycles of high-dose cytarabine (3 g/m² BID for 3 days = 30,240 mg total per cycle). Response: Complete remission (CR). MRD negativity in bone marrow (flow cytometry and NPM1 PCR) achieved after the first consolidation cycle.
September 14, 2024: Patient noted palpable swellings in the chest. Gynecologic evaluation showed bead-like lymph nodes on ultrasound. Biopsy confirmed AML infiltration. Repeated bone marrow biopsy demonstrated ongoing CR but increased NPM1 levels (ratio 1.42). PET-CT was not performed. Donor search initiated.
Salvage therapy (September 20, 2024): Venetoclax 400 mg days 1–28 plus azacitidine 75 mg/m² days 1–7. September 28, 2024: Hospitalization due to neutropenic fever. Meropenem 1 g TID was initiated for 7 days (21 g total). Posaconazole 300 mg/day was started on day 13 of cycle 1, and venetoclax was reduced to 70 mg for the last 14 days (total dose 6,180 mg). Patient was discharged on October 8, 2024. After one cycle, extramedullary lesions were no longer palpable. Neutrophils recovered on October 18, 2024. PET-CT confirmed no further delineable lesions.
Second cycle (November 2, 2024): Venetoclax 70 mg days 1–14 (due to comedication with posaconazole 300 mg), achieving complete remission.
Allogeneic SCT (December 7, 2024): Haploidentical sibling donor; conditioning with fludarabine 30 mg/m² for 5 days, treosulfan 14 g/m² for 3 days, and thiotepa 5 mg/kg twice on day -2. GVHD prophylaxis: Post-transplant cyclophosphamide (50 mg/kg on days +3 and +4), mycophenolate mofetil (MMF), and tacrolimus.
Complications: Febrile episodes with suspected central venous catheter infection. Empiric antibiotic therapy was initiated with piperacillin/tazobactam 4.5 g TID for 3 days, later escalated to meropenem 1 g i.v. TID for 13 days, and vancomycin 1 g BID (later increased to 1.5 g-0-1.25 g for 5 days) for Gram-positive coverage. Hemolysis was suspected (haptoglobin <10 mg/dL, schistocytes 6‰ on peripheral smear, and LDH 324 U/L, bilirubin 1.2 mg/dL) in the setting of ABO-incompatible transplantation. The patient received rituximab 375 mg/m² i.v. weekly × 4 doses (total 2,520 mg).
January 10, 2025: Complete remission (CR); 100% donor chimerism; no MRD detectable in bone marrow.
March 28, 2025 (day +100): PET-CT showed no FDG-avid lesions. Day +107: Patient developed skin GvHD grade III°, steroid-refractory (Days 1–7: prednisolone 120 mg daily, tapered by 10 mg every 3 days) but responsive to ruxolitinib 10 mg BID. GvHD overlapped into mild to moderate chronic GvHD, controlled with ruxolitinib 10 mg BID.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Relapsed NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia with extramedullary involvement (myeloid sarcoma) after allogeneic stem cell transplantation", "differentials": ["Post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD)", "Viral infection (EBV, CMV) with cutaneous and cardiac manifestations", "Drug reaction (e.g., ruxolitinib-induced skin eruption and pericarditis)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Brain MRI with contrast to evaluate for CNS involvement; lumbar puncture for CSF cytology, flow cytometry, and NPM1 PCR", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Cardiac MRI with contrast to characterize pericardial/septal mass and assess myocardial involvement", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Induction chemotherapy with FLAG-IDA (fludarabine, cytarabine, G-CSF, idarubicin) to achieve remission", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider intrathecal chemotherapy prophylaxis (methotrexate/cytarabine/hydrocortisone) if CNS involvement is excluded; if positive, therapeutic intrathecal chemotherapy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Consultation with radiation oncology for consideration of localized radiotherapy to symptomatic or bulky extramedullary lesions (skin, abdominal mass, cardiac mass if causing symptoms)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) after achieving remission, with careful monitoring for GVHD and potential taper of immunosuppression (including ruxolitinib)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Supportive care: infection prophylaxis (antibacterial, antifungal, antiviral), G-CSF, transfusion support, and close monitoring for tumor lysis syndrome", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Relapsed NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia with extramedullary involvement (myeloid sarcoma) after allogeneic stem cell transplantation
differentials: Viral infection (EBV, CMV) with cutaneous and cardiac manifestations
Drug reaction (e.g., ruxolitinib-induced skin eruption and pericarditis)

treatment_plan.action: Brain MRI with contrast to evaluate for CNS involvement; lumbar puncture for CSF cytology, flow cytometry, and NPM1 PCR
Cardiac MRI with contrast to characterize pericardial/septal mass and assess myocardial involvement
Induction chemotherapy with FLAG-IDA (fludarabine, cytarabine, G-CSF, idarubicin) to achieve remission
Consider intrathecal chemotherapy prophylaxis (methotrexate/cytarabine/hydrocortisone) if CNS involvement is excluded; if positive, therapeutic intrathecal chemotherapy
Consultation with radiation oncology for consideration of localized radiotherapy to symptomatic or bulky extramedullary lesions (skin, abdominal mass, cardiac mass if causing symptoms)
Donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) after achieving remission, with careful monitoring for GVHD and potential taper of immunosuppression (including ruxolitinib)
Supportive care: infection prophylaxis (antibacterial, antifungal, antiviral), G-CSF, transfusion support, and close monitoring for tumor lysis syndrome
